repo_notes.content: i am editing wiki first time

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce OpenHands, explaining what it is, its main capabilities, and high-level architecture overview

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.purpose: Getting users up and running quickly with basic setup and first conversation

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Detailed explanation of the overall system architecture, components, and how they interact

pages.title: Core Components
pages.purpose: Overview of the main system components and how they work together

pages.title: Agent System
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the agent architecture, session management, and execution lifecycle

pages.title: Runtime System
pages.purpose: Explanation of the pluggable runtime environments for secure code execution

pages.title: LLM Integration
pages.purpose: How the system integrates with various language models, configuration, and metrics

pages.title: Event System
pages.purpose: The event-driven communication backbone that connects all components

pages.title: Function Calling
pages.purpose: How agents interact with tools through function calling and tool integration

pages.title: User Interfaces
pages.purpose: Overview of the different ways users can interact with OpenHands

pages.title: Web Frontend
pages.purpose: The React-based web interface, real-time communication, and user experience

pages.title: Command Line Interface
pages.purpose: Using OpenHands through CLI commands and headless mode

pages.title: API Reference
pages.purpose: REST API endpoints and WebSocket communication for developers

pages.title: Configuration
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide to configuring OpenHands for different use cases

pages.title: Development
pages.purpose: Guide for developers contributing to or extending OpenHands

pages.title: Building and Dependencies
pages.purpose: Setting up development environment, dependency management, and build process

pages.title: Testing and CI/CD
pages.purpose: Automated testing, continuous integration, and deployment pipelines

pages.title: Docker and Deployment
pages.purpose: Container images, deployment strategies, and infrastructure setup

pages.title: Evaluation System
pages.purpose: How to run benchmarks and evaluate agent performance

pages.title: Integrations
pages.purpose: External service integrations and automation capabilities

pages.title: Git Provider Integration
pages.purpose: Integration with GitHub, GitLab, and other Git providers

pages.title: GitHub Actions Automation
pages.purpose: Automated issue resolution and PR creation through GitHub Actions

pages.title: Reference
pages.purpose: Additional utilities, legacy components, and technical reference material

pages.title: Utilities and Helpers
pages.purpose: Utility functions, path resolution, and helper components
